title: The History of Maps
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text:
Maps have been essential tools for human navigation and understanding of the world (0). thousands of years. The earliest known maps, dating back to ancient Babylon, were simple clay tablets that (1). local areas. As civilisations expanded, the need for more accurate representations of geography became increasingly (2). .

During the Age of Exploration, mapmaking (3). tremendous advances. Cartographers worked closely with sailors who (4). back information about newly discovered lands. These maps were often kept as closely guarded secrets, as they (5). valuable strategic advantages to the nations that possessed them.

The twentieth century (6). about revolutionary changes in how maps were created. Aerial photography and later satellite imaging made it possible to produce incredibly detailed and accurate maps. Today, digital mapping applications have (7). the way we navigate. Anyone with a smartphone can access detailed maps of virtually anywhere on Earth. Despite these technological advances, traditional paper maps still (8). a certain charm that many travellers appreciate.

example.explanation: 'For' se usa con períodos de tiempo para indicar duración.

questions.explanation: 'Depict' es el verbo formal correcto para representar en mapas o imágenes.
'Become apparent' es la colocación correcta para hacerse evidente.
'Make advances' es la colocación correcta para lograr avances.
'Bring back' significa traer de vuelta información o cosas.
'Provide advantages' es la colocación correcta para proporcionar ventajas.
'Bring about changes' significa provocar o causar cambios.
'Transform the way' es la colocación correcta para cambiar radicalmente algo.
'Hold a charm' significa conservar o tener cierto encanto.
